labeling.method: transcript-window matching (+/-3s, >=2 tokens, >=60% hit) + y-position and numeral guards; layout frame-verified
honesty: Recall cell: real word-timed creator captions must label likely_caption — the half of the pack that punishes over-aggressive filters.
Cards/title graphics deliberately echo narration; y<0.5 guard separates them; the 'VISION PRO' bottom-card label resolved by frame verification, not the rule.
'METALWOOD' wobble cluster: persistent in-scene text, source object unidentified in reviewed frames — incidental on persistence + non-caption timing.
Numeral-heavy lines are card/screen text (real), never 'unreadable' — an earlier draft of these labels got that wrong and was corrected before commit.

The 402 unreadable rows are single-character wobble reads concentrated at the outlined-caption/card band (y~0.6): they originate from REAL typography, but the read itself carries no recoverable text — 'unreadable' describes the read, not the source.
